---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Cmd+Shift+Enter*. 
`
```{r}
#loading dataset
df <- read.csv("ResaleflatpricesbasedonregistrationdatefromJan2017onwards.csv")
head(df)
```

```{r}
#understanding what columns we have
names(df)
```

```{r}
#understanding column types
str(df)
```
```{r}
#understanding size of data
dim(df)
#227735 rows and 11 columns
```
 
```{r}
#checking for missing values (if any)
sum(is.na(df))
#no missing values
```
 
```{r}
#understanding statistical distributions of numerical features 
#that makes sense to analyse
#floor_area_sqm
summary(df$floor_area_sqm)
```
 
```{r}
#resale_price
summary(df$resale_price)
```
 
```{r}
#encode categorical variables
#work with copy of df:
df_v1 <- df

#label encoding 

#town
town_mapping <- levels(factor(df_v1$town))
df_v1$town <- as.integer(factor(df_v1$town))

#flat_type
flat_type_mapping <- levels(factor(df$flat_type))
df_v1$flat_type <- as.integer(factor(df_v1$flat_type))

#storey_range
storey_range_mapping <- levels(factor(df_v1$storey_range))
df_v1$storey_range <- as.integer(factor(df_v1$storey_range))

#flat model
flat_model_mapping <- levels(factor(df_v1$flat_model))
df_v1$flat_model <- as.integer(factor(df_v1$flat_model))

head(df_v1)



```
 
 
```{r}
#feature extraction & engineering

#extract months from remaining lease
extract_months <- function(lease_str){
  numbers <- as.integer(regmatches(lease_str,gregexpr("\\d+",lease_str))[[1]])
  if (length(numbers)==2){
    return(numbers[1]*12+numbers[2])
  }else if (length(numbers)==1){
    if (grep("year",tolower(lease_str))){
      return (numbers[1]*12)
    }else{
      return(numbers[1])
    }
  }
  return[0]
}
df_v1$remaining_lease <- sapply(df_v1$remaining_lease,extract_months)

#create time index column + month extraction (to capture trends across the years)
library(lubridate)
df_v1$month <- ym(df_v1$month)
min_date <- min(df_v1$month)
df_v1$time_index <- (year(df_v1$month)-year(min_date))*12 + (month(df_v1$month)-month(min_date))
df_v1$month_num <- month(df_v1$month)

tail(df_v1,10)
```
 
```{r}
#modelling begins
#we will also calculate average resale price per street, however, we will do so only on training data to prevent leakage

set.seed(42)
train_idx <- sample(nrow(df_v1),0.8*nrow(df_v1))
train_df_v1 <- df_v1[train_idx,]
test_df_v1 <- df_v1[-train_idx,]

street_map <- tapply(train_df_v1$resale_price,train_df_v1$street_name,mean)
                                                                                                                                      train_df_v1$street_avg_price <- street_map[train_df_v1$street_name]                                                                                     
test_df_v1$street_avg_price  <- street_map[test_df_v1$street_name]

head(train_df_v1)
```
 
```{r}
tail(train_df_v1)
```
 
```{r}
#feature selection
features <- c('town','flat_type','floor_area_sqm','storey_range','flat_model','remaining_lease','time_index','month_num','street_avg_price')
#train and test datasets to work with for modeling
X_train = train_df_v1[,features]
y_train = train_df_v1$resale_price

X_test = test_df_v1[,features]
y_test =test_df_v1$resale_price
```

```{r}
head(X_train)
```
```{r}
#xgboost regressor modelling begins
install.packages("xgboost")
library(xgboost)

#convert to matrix format
X_train_mat <- as.matrix(X_train)
X_test_mat <- as.matrix(X_test)

#convert to Dmatrix form for XGBoost 
dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data=X_train_mat,label=y_train)
dtest <- xgb.DMatrix(data=X_test_mat,label=y_test)

xgbreg_model <- xgb.train(
  params = list(
    objective="reg:squarederror",
    eta=0.1,
    #default xgboost depth
    max_depth=6
  ),
  data = dtrain,
  nrounds = 3000,
  evals = list(train=dtrain,test=dtest),
  early_stopping_rounds = 20,
  verbose = 1
)

#check what is the best n of nrounds
cat("Best nrounds:",xgbreg_model$best_iteration,"\n")
predictions <- predict(xgbreg_model,dtest)

#mean absolute error
mae <- mean(abs(y_test-predictions))
#residual sum of squares
res_ss <- sum((y_test-predictions)^2)

#R square value
r2 <- 1-res_ss/sum((y_test-mean(y_test))^2)

cat("MAE: $",round(mae,2),"\n")
cat("R^2:",round(r2,4),"\n")
```
```{r}
cat("MAE as % of mean price:",round(mae / mean(y_test) * 100, 2),"%\n")
```
```{r}

```

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Cmd+Option+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Cmd+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file). 

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
